TREE DOWN ACROSS NATCHEZ TRACE PARKWAY
PINEY RIVER IS SWOLEN CAUSING 4 TO 8 INCHES OF WATER TO RUN ACROSS TN HWY 46 IN SEVERAL SPOTS NORTH OF INTERSTATE 40.
TREE FELL ONTO APARTMENT BUILDING AND CAUSED MINOR SIDING DAMAGE

NUMEROUS ROADS WERE FLOODED WITH UP TO 1 TO 2 FEET OF WATER ACROSS SOUTHERN DICKSON COUNTY INCLUDING EAST PINEY ROAD...HOGAN ROAD...TURKEY CREEK ROAD...AND GUM BRANCH ROAD. SEVERAL WATER RESCUES WERE CONDUCTED INCLUDING 4 PEOPLE RESCUED FROM A HOUSE ON GUM BRANCH ROAD AND 5 PEOPLE RESCUED FROM A HOUSE ON TURKEY CREEK ROAD. ONE UNOCCUPIED CAR ON TURKEY CREEK ROAD WAS WASHED 500 YARDS DOWNSTREAM. AT LEAST ONE DOZEN HOMES WERE IMPACTED BY FLOODING.
WAREHOUSE FLOODED AND THE MCDONALDS IS FLOODED...CARS STUCK
SOME TREES DOWN ACROSS CENTRAL PORTIONS OF THE COUNTY

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE IN SOUTHEAST PERRY COUNTY FROM AN EF1 TORNADO. THE TORNADO UPROOTED...SNAPPED OR DOWNED HUNDREDS OF TREES ALONG ITS PATH BEFORE ENTERING LEWIS COUNTY. THE TORNADO WEAKENED TO AN EF0 THERE AND DAMAGED AN OUT BUILDING BEFORE LIFTING 7 MILES WEST OF HOHENWALD. PATH WIDTH WAS 350 YARDS AND STRENGTH WAS 90 MPH AT ITS PEAK. TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND EF0 ANTICYCLONIC TORNADO DAMAGE IN NORTHWEST LEWIS COUNTY. A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG DARBYTOWN ROAD WITH SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES DOWNED AS WELL. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A BARN AND HOUSE ALONG ITS ONE MILE PATH. ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 80 MPH. PATH WIDTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 50 YARDS.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WERE THE CAUSE FOR DAMAGE IN WESTERN MAURY COUNTY. AN OLD BARD WAS DESTROYED ALONG CECIL FARM ROAD ALONG WITH DOZENS OF TREES UPROOTED OR DOWNED ALONG CROSS BRIDGE RD. SWATH OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WAS ABOUT 1 MILE AND WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH.

AN OLD BARN WAS DESTROYED ALONG CECIL FARM ROAD ALONG WITH DOZENS OF TREES UPROOTED OR DOWNED ALONG CROSS BRIDGE RD. SWATH OF STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WAS ABOUT 1 MILE AND WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH.

ANOTHER HOME LOST MOST OF ITS WEST FACING SHINGLES ON DANA DRIVE...WHICH WERE BLOWN UP TO 150 YARDS EASTWARD. ANOTHER HOME WAS DAMAGED BY A FALLEN TREE ON WESTVIEW DR. A BOAT DOCK WAS BLOWN 130 YARDS TO THE NE. DOZENS OF LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED FROM DANA DR AND NORTHVIEW CIR TO NEAR HWY 109. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 80 MPH.
